analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Fall25. I am not a polysci major, but I loved this class as a gen ed. Tavana navigates well through heated discussion on the Middle East. The class challenges you to memorize a lot of information and form opinions based on it. The only issue I had was the weighting is very heavy on exams, and it is hard to get a 100% on group projects.

Professor Tavana is very nice and wants his students to succeed. I did struggle with his teaching style at times. He wanted students to ask questions or go over things they found confusing however when students did he would say that he just went over that or that we should know it. A bit difficult for a 200 level course but still ale to do well.

Dr. Tavana is a great professor, and it's obvious that he knows the Middle East very well. He can have high expectations, but is very clear about what he expects and is generally pretty accommodating, and wants to help students as much as he can. He is also pretty easy to reach outside of class and more than willing to schedule office hours.

Daniel Tavana was an absolutely great professor! His Middle East politics class was a more difficult course for me, but he taught the content very well. His knowledge of these topics is very thorough, and he fosters independence through his teaching methods. I left this class knowing much more than I did about the Middle East than before!

Great professor who has a wealth of knowledge on the subject. Exams are very easy if you even study a little bit or come to class. Almost no work outside of class with the exception of 2 group projects that are not time-consuming. Would highly recommend taking this as a Gen Ed interdomain.

analysis.name: Amy Thul-Sigler
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This class was horrible for me. It consists of 3 major assignments (modified business plan) that determine whether you fail or pass. All other assignments mean nothing because these 3 are worth most of your grade. Inconsistent grading/ZERO guidance on any of it, just told to complete it/"I should already know." I changed my major due to this class!
Yes it's a tough course but I learned more than I expected. Follow the rubrics and take advantage of internship postings.

HPA 390 online: Easy A or B course that offers assignments that you will use in the work field. This is a pre-internship course that consists of writing memos, resume, personal statement, cover letter & a organizational research paper. It sounds like a lot but it's not. No exams. The professor is helpful & answers questions about internships.
Do all the assignments. Shes a super easy grader on the daily logs and presentation. Just learn from your internship experience

analysis.name: Tendai Masaya
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Tough class, but he genuinely tries to help. I had to retake it; before I dropped, he even offered to give me a C+. I retook it and earned a B+. He adds solid curves, especially on the final, but don't rely on them. The study guide and note problems are really good, the test questions are almost identical, so redo them as studying.

The class itself can be tough but if you just show up to class and work alongside him on the course packets for each chapter, the class should be a breeze. If you do get lost, don't be afraid to raise your hand and ask questions. He does give extra credit at the end of the semester.

472 is hard material but he made it seem a little worse than it had to be. His exams were pretty difficult (based mainly off the material), but his practice exams made it a bit easier. Not the best professor but may have been because of the difficulty level of the material. He seems to care about what he's teaching.

Greatest professor in ACCTG 472. Explain every question in detail and provide practice problems before each exam in class and office hour, even in the email. Pay attention the teaching style and strategy and listen to students' opinions. Has a group project on writing paper but not difficult, just follow the guideline and you will get high score.
Great teacher. He relies on the course packet instead of just reading off slides which makes everything easier to follow. His exam reviews are helpful for the tests. Would recommend.

analysis.name: Todd Price
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He taught me Cyber100, and he's absolutely wonderful. Professor Price is always so helpful, down to earth, hilarious, and care for his students a lot. He hands out a lot of EC which helps a ton. He's always cracking jokes and posts weekly about seminars and internships. The best professor ever easily.

Dr. Price is the best professor. I had him for Cyber 100 and could not have made the class any easier. It's just online quizzes every week along with a lab every week but they are mostly done in class and are very easy. Definitely take a class with Dr. Price.

Dr. Price is one of the best professors I have taken. He is very hilarious and can make a boring class into a fun class. He really cares about his students and will help you as much as he can. There is a lab and an easy online quiz each 2 weeks. He cares about feedback from students. I would really recommend taking any course that he teaches.
Saved our class. Shows he cares for the students
Best professor I ever had at Penn State!! I was nervous going into the class as I am a business major, but he was super helpful with assignments and asked for our feedback for exams.

analysis.name: Timothy Shick
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Didn't listen to students' questions and talked over them. Wasn't clear in expectation.

He expects you to buy the books vs others who offer them for free, his books were around $200, lots of papers, one per week and the days you didn't write a paper are group projects. Final essay was 2500 words vs in my LHR 100 final assigment was to write what we learned.. would not take again.

I took Dr. Shick for HRER 826 and had a great experience. He was always available and provided great feedback on the assignments submitted. He also did zoom meetings and provided examples on how things can be down which was extremely helpful! The class did consist of a huge group project that was done throughout the semester. Definitely recommend!

Prof Shick is a great communicator. Material easy to follow and navigate. Negative side if a large portion of your grade is based upon a semester long group project with randomly assigned people which is never enjoyable in college. Overall decent professor, easy material, lots of homework, strict framework.

I recommend Professor Shick. You do not have any test to take at all. You just have group debates and 5 essays to submit for the whole semester. You get to chose whichever essays you want throughout the semester. He is a fair grader and great feedback. He responds back in a timely fashion to his emails. Which he could be my professor all the time.

analysis.name: Judith Udechukwu

review_highlights.comment: There were tons of lectures, textbook readings, and lectures that felt unnecessary. She graded unclearly, too. The assignments weren't properly addressed/explained at times, so I had to give it my best shot and hope she wouldn't take off points. I usually enjoy public speaking, but she made the class not very enjoyable.

Gives the bare minimum explanation for all assignments and expects you to be able to figure out what she wants from you. When she does give instructions, they're extremely unclear and just cause more confusion. Loves to not put assignments on the calendar or to-do. Rambles during lecture and says very little that actually pertains to the class.

Not as harsh of a grader as it seems, she'll give scary feedback but then give you an A on your speech. The feedback is also useful to improve on your next speech. You can only miss three classes per semester, but I think that's pretty standard across all CAS100 courses. Definitely not bad to the point where you should drop if she's your professor.

A public speaking teacher that stands at the front of the class and reads slides for fifty minutes. Listen to her feedback; she will grade you on your improvement specifically in those areas. She often is unprepared for class, but seemingly expects a perfect attendance from her students. Not the greatest learning experience, would not take again.

One of the worst professors I have ever had. She is consistently unprepared and puts the bare minimum into teaching, yet expects the maximum from you. Her teaching in this class genuinely made me wonder if she was qualified to teach this at all. She will assign work with little instructions and grade with little explanation. Would not recommend

analysis.name: Alp Uzman
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently

review_highlights.comment: University of Utah Foundations of Analysis 2. I got an A+ in Foundations 1, but barely grasp it now. Cons: Lectures seem extremely disorganized. Does not follow the textbook, so if you don't understand what he's teaching, you're lost. The homework questions are ridiculously hard. Pros: records lectures and posts them on Youtube. Grading is lenient.

I am rating this professor again because I gave him a 1/5 when I was upset, so now I am giving him a 3/5 because overall I would say he was a 2/5. I didn't love his teaching style, but his grading was very generous which was his saving grace.

Uzman at the University of Utah is a fantastic teacher. His lectures are great and you can tell he is absolutely brilliant. The homework can get quite difficult, however, the exams were very easy comparatively. Overall, one of my favorite math classes so far.

He is definitely the worst math "professor" (grad student) I've had at Penn State and if you know anything about them they all aren't good. He didn't teach us for half the semester when covid happened. The final practice exams went from 12 questions to having 24 and no one finished. He also gave an exam the day after THON worth 20% of our grade.

Knows his stuff but he doesn't explain it well sometimes. However, he's a good and funny dude. All grades came from exams and quizzes. Didn't teach us directly during COVID but provided lecture videos which was better IMO. Sometimes he uses weird notation and goes way too in depth, which is cool but confusing. Overall, not the best but far from bad

analysis.name: Juan Udaondo Alegre
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The most caring and understanding teacher. Get ready to read as that is the main homework along with responding to questions but its always the same structure which makes it easy and not stressful.

One of the best classes I have taken so far. As a native spanish speaker I was still challenged by the material and the discussions were amazing. It is not too hard, even for non-native speakers, as long as you participate and show enthusiasm for the literature. The books chosen were really good, and I loved the class and the teacher.

Juan is a caring professor. We covered lots of material on the textbook, but he made sure we understood everything. Apart from readings and comprehension questions... be ready to participate. This class will for sure help you learn while encouraging you out of your comfort zone.

Great professor! There is a lot of reading and homework, but you review the summary in class. Two papers and two tests that are mostly composed of free response questions. He's super nice and approachable. During COVID-19, he was very prepared for zoom classes and kept them engaging.

My favorite Spanish professor at Penn State. Although this class required a lot of reading, I learned how to become a better analyzer while also improving my Spanish. You can tell Juan cares about what he's teaching which makes class more engaging. The SEEQ closed before our class ended, and I wasn't able to complete one :( Take his class!

analysis.name: Jeffery Ulmer
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Professor Ulmer is a nice guy, and he will help you out if you need it. There are 4 exams, and each tests you on the lectures (PowerPoint) and the book; exams are not hard if you study. Clicker questions are used for attendance, and are scored based off of participation, not accuracy of the answer. Overall a good class and a good professor!

Professor Ulmer is a solid option for this course. You'll have 4 papers and 1 longer final paper throughout the semester, along with 3 exams and no final exam. He can be a little confusing during lectures since he has a tendency to say pickup words like “ya know” every 5 seconds, but he gives out study guides that are usually spot on for exams.

You would think that someone with a PhD would have enough intelligence to accurately teach information about his field. Nope. Dr. Ulmer is a horrible instructor and spends every class reading word for word off the slides or rambling about some irrelevant side story. Also, theres so little points in this course so one bad grade screws you over.

Professor Ulmer is a very nice guy, but I wouldn't take him again. This class was too much for a 200 level. There was only 500 points in a semester, so if you mess up one thing it really affects your grade. You have to write 5 papers throughout the year, and the last one is 10 pages. 3 exams and the mean was usually a 70%. No final though.
Extremely boring class. He reads exactly from the PowerPoint and will go over class time because he is too busy speaking. Attendance IS mandatory.

analysis.name: Urjaswala Vora

review_highlights.comment: By far the worst professor I have ever had in my time at Penn State. She is impossible to understand and her grading criteria are unclear. Her lectures are extremely boring and you can't follow along to what she is saying pretty much all the time. She will meet with you but she is no help. I seriously do not understand how she is still employed.

Professor Vora is a very sweet person but her teaching style is very jumpy and disjointed. Was corrected by students many times and often doesn't explain what she's doing when coding which can be really confusing. Also often misunderstand student's questions and provides unrelated answers. Sweet person but really frustrating class to learn in.

They didn't post the final or even made it available to be seen until the day it was due for an online course. If you are taking an online course you have a full time job and have to plan your assignments ahead of time and that is what the calendar in Canvas is for. She decided to not post it and I never saw it dropping me from an A to a B

Professor Vora is nice, but she doesn't really know how to teach a class well. Classes are spent with her just writing code and you copying it because she doesn't teach any of the concepts very well. If you go to class, she will basically give you the answers to most of the assignments. It's also difficult to understand her sometimes.

Absolute waste of a class. Each assignment was the equivalent of doing one leetcode problem once a week and have another person posting video lectures that are not thought out at all prior to them beginning it. This legit feels like a 100 level class with a student teaching it. I am online student btw. Do not take a class with this professor

How do I feel now you ask? I feel nothing but the soreness of covid vaccine faintly from my upper arm.

I know CS class is time consuming but this is really bad and my CS311 and CS331 are both A and I think I may not pass this class (I have never failed a class)

The exams were way too challenging and needed big curves to get people to pass. Bhuvan is very knowledgeable, but the lectures got boring and he was not of big help in office hours.,

This man told me to repeat a class that I got an A in. He said this to me because I spoke out on zoom when he expected us to know something that we were not explicitly taught. This was a terribly unorganized class.

This is a hard class. But Bhuvan is very knowledgable and you must reach out to him and the TAs for help. Coding projects are insanely hard, the exams are also pretty bad. But he curves a lot, so if you are in the average, you still will be fine. Make sure to start as soon as the project is released and ask for a lot of help.

2 MID TERMS + FINAL btw
